2603.18620.title: Learning to Self-Evolve
2603.18620.paper_summary: We introduce Learning to Self-Evolve (LSE), a reinforcement learning framework that trains large language models (LLMs) to improve their own contexts at test time. We situate LSE in the setting of test-time self-evolution, where a model iteratively refines its context from feedback on seen problems to perform better on new ones. Existing approaches rely entirely on the inherent reasoning ability of the model and never explicitly train it for this task. LSE reduces the multi-step evolution problem to a single-step RL objective, where each context edit is rewarded by the improvement in downstream performance. We pair this objective with a tree-guided evolution loop. On Text-to-SQL generation (BIRD) and general question answering (MMLU-Redux), a 4B-parameter model trained with LSE outperforms self-evolving policies powered by GPT-5 and Claude Sonnet 4.5, as well as prompt optimization methods including GEPA and TextGrad, and transfers to guide other models without additional training. Our results highlight the effectiveness of treating self-evolution as a learnable skill.
2603.18620.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了自我進化學習（Learning to Self-Evolve, LSE），這是一個強化學習框架，旨在訓練大型語言模型（LLMs）在測試時改善其上下文。我們將LSE置於測試時自我進化的背景下，在這裡，模型通過對已見問題的反饋迭代地完善其上下文，以便在新問題上表現得更好。現有的方法完全依賴於模型固有的推理能力，並且從未明確地訓練它來執行這項任務。LSE將多步進化問題簡化為單步強化學習目標，每次上下文編輯都根據下游性能的改善來獲得獎勵。我們將這一目標與樹導向的進化循環相結合。在文本到SQL生成（BIRD）和一般問題回答（MMLU-Redux）中，使用LSE訓練的4B參數模型超越了由GPT-5和Claude Sonnet 4.5驅動的自我進化策略，以及包括GEPA和TextGrad在內的提示優化方法，並能夠在不進行額外訓練的情況下指導其他模型。我們的結果凸顯了將自我進化視為可學習技能的有效性。
2603.18620.author: Xiaoyin Chen et.al.
2603.18620.authors: Xiaoyin Chen, Canwen Xu, Yite Wang, Boyi Liu, Zhewei Yao, Yuxiong He
